Defendants' Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Their Respective Answers
Off Calendar Oct-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS PAUL RYAN, JAMES FRIEDMAN and ORBIS TERTIUS, LLC'S Motion For Leave To Amend Their Respective Answers:

Defendants' motion for leave to amend their answers to the second amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the policy of liberality in amendment of pleadings. "In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense." (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159.) Although Defendants delayed seeking leave to amend, Plaintiffs have been on notice since June 2021 that Defendants intended to amend their answers, and Plaintiffs do not show that they would be prejudiced by the amendments, which seek to add two affirmative legal defenses (res judicata and barred by mandatory arbitration agreement) that are not new to the case and are unlikely to require additional discovery.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

OTHER MARR B. OLSEN, INC. MOTION TO INTERVENE

Marr B. Olson, Inc.'s motion to intervene is denied. Whether intervention is of right, or only permissive, the party seeking leave to intervene must make "timely" application to the court. (CCP 387.) Trial of this case is set for October 18, 2021 - only 24 days from now, and Olson's proposed complaint pleads four causes of action. Olson's counsel attributes her delay to unreturned phone calls, but the existence of this case (filed in 2019) could have been determined from public court websites in a matter of minutes.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312
